refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving a productivity target) without specifying how. It is atomic because it addresses a single obligation: supporting productivity target achievement. The units (m³/h or ton/h) are part of the measurement context, not implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection that the NGHE solution enables achieving or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target over the agreed evaluation period under representative conditions.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in mass per hour.

candidate_dps: Adjust processing rate parameter to meet productivity target
Allocate resources dynamically to maintain throughput
Optimize task sequencing to maximize output
Monitor and control operational parameters affecting productivity
Implement feedback control loop to adjust system behavior based on productivity measurements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement feedback control loop to adjust system behavior based on productivity measurements

potential_sources: Feedback control loop operates independently from other DPs if designed modularly
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP encapsulates productivity control logic, minimizing coupling with other system functions by isolating adjustment mechanisms within a feedback loop.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely measurement of productivity is available
Operational policies allow dynamic adjustment of system parameters
Feedback control loop can respond within the evaluation period to maintain target productivity
Risk that external factors (e.g., supply chain delays) may impact productivity beyond control of the DP
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including measurement and data acquisition as separate DPs to ensure modularity and clarity in the control feedback loop.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic including control algorithms and productivity measurement processing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives productivity measurements and system state data.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends control commands to adjust system behavior based on feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with other modules and external systems for feedback data and control signals.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as feedback control logic and productivity measurement processing are core functions of NGHE Solution. Minor diffusion through interfaces to other modules.
Vehicle module executes control commands but does not contain feedback control logic itself, causing diffusion of control logic outside.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring, not on autonomous feedback control, leading to poor containment.
Safety Services is focused on safety alerts and monitoring, unrelated to feedback control for productivity, resulting in very weak containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The feedback control loop for adjusting system behavior based on productivity measurements is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, as it centralizes control logic and data processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for execution of control commands may cause some diffusion of control logic.
Dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce complexity in interface management.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement feedback control loop to adjust system behavior based on productivity measurements' directly addresses the FR of supporting achievement of the customer-defined productivity target by providing a mechanism to adjust system behavior based on measured productivity. This mapping is logically sound and grounded in the acceptance criteria requiring productivity to meet or exceed the target.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for execution of control commands may cause some diffusion of control logic outside the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with core feedback control logic and productivity measurement processing centralized, minimizing diffusion. Minor diffusion exists due to interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, but containment scores and notes indicate this is managed and acceptable.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives productivity measurements via Telemetry and adjusts system behavior through Tasking commands, enabling monitoring of productivity against targets during operations.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the feedback control loop implementation, aligning the FR and DP with a well-defined IM boundary, supporting clear allocation and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage the interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to reduce diffusion risk, possibly by defining clearer interface contracts or consolidating control command execution logic.
Document and verify the dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to ensure robust communication and minimize integration risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong logical mappings, acceptable diffusion risks, and clear ownership boundaries. Verification feasibility is confirmed, and the recommended revisions focus on monitoring and managing interfaces rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, centralized feedback control loop within the NGHE Solution module is prudent to minimize complexity and risk. Therefore, no further decomposition into sub-FRs is warranted at this stage.

revise.why: To address minor diffusion risks and integration dependencies, revise the implementation module by refining interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules and documenting dependencies on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces for robust communication.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the top-level FR regarding productivity target achievement.
The NGHE Solution module can effectively encapsulate the feedback control loop with manageable interface complexity.
Verification methods are sufficient to monitor productivity and system behavior adjustments during operation.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of control logic through interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Integration risks related to Telemetry and Network Link communication dependencies.
evidence_needed: Interface contract definitions and documentation between NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation modules.
Verification test results demonstrating effective feedback control loop operation and productivity target achievement.
Risk assessments on communication dependencies and mitigation strategies.
